data.title: Rexford Industrial Realty appoints board chairman
data.description:
Rexford Industrial Realty (NYSE:REXR) announced on Monday the retirement of board chairman Richard Ziman following the industrial REIT's June 3 annual meeting of stockholders.

Current lead independent director Tyler Rose is set to serve as the board chairman, effective as of the conclusion of the annual meeting.

The appointment is subject to the results of the annual meeting and the election of directors.

data.title: FB Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.02, revenue of $130.67M misses by $1.19M
data.description:
• FB Financial press release (NYSE:FBK): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.85 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $130.67M (+21.6% Y/Y) misses by $1.19M.

data.title: NeuroPace expects minimal impact of tariffs on its operations and financial results
data.description:
• NeuroPace (NASDAQ:NPCE), on Monday, provided an update on its exposure to tariffs that have been implemented. While specific policies and timing of implementation remain fluid, NeuroPace expects minimal impact to its operations and financial results.
  • Specifically, NeuroPace manufactures and sells the vast majority of its devices in the United States and has limited supply chain activities outside the United States for its RNS System. 
  • Similarly, the company expects no material impact on gross margin for DIXI Medical SEEG products. As a result, NeuroPace expects minimal impact to its gross margin for fiscal year 2025 and is reiterating its gross margin guidance for 2025. 
  • NeuroPace previously announced terminating its SEEG distribution agreement with DIXI Medical effective October 1, 2025, followed by a six-month wind down period through the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Marex announces public offering of 8.5M shares
data.description:
Financial services platform Marex Group (NASDAQ:MRX) announced on Monday the launch of a public offering of 8.5M ordinary shares by certain selling shareholders.

The company said it is not selling any shares in the offering.

Additionally, the selling shareholders have granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to 1.28M shares.

MRX shares were -1.95% post-market to $37.73.

data.title: Soleno launches Vykat xr for hyperphagia in Prader-Willi syndrome
data.description:
• Soleno Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SLNO) announced the U.S. commercial availability of Vykat xr extended-release tablets for the treatment for hyperphagia in patients four years of age and older with Prader-Willi syndrome.
  • Vykat xr was approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration on March 26, 2025.
  • Prescriptions of Vykat xr have now been delivered to the first individuals living with PWS who have been prescribed the medication, the company said.

data.title: Applied Digital Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 beats by $0.02, revenue of $52.92M misses by $9.99M
data.description:
• Applied Digital press release (NASDAQ:APLD): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $52.92M (+22.2% Y/Y) misses by $9.99M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $10 million

data.title: YPF upgraded at HSBC as Argentina takes big step towards economic normalization
data.description:
YPF (NYSE:YPF) +9.5% in Monday's trading as HSBC upgrades shares of Argentina's state-run oil producer to Hold from Reduce with a $33 price target, raised from $21, citing improved country, sector and company-specific fundamentals.

Argentina's government and the International Monetary Fund confirmed late Friday a $20B, four-year extended fund facility, including the lifting of nearly all capital controls, elimination of export foreign exchange blend, and flexibilization of forex access for imports, with introduction of a forex floating regime with a band whose initial ceiling is ~30% weaker than the prevailing official exchange rate, which is "a strong positive that in our view leads to a narrower required return gap for Argentinean equities relative to peers," HSBC's Lilyanna Yang writes.

In addition to Argentina's improved macroeconomic outlook, Yang says she is much more constructive on the country's commitment to develop its energy sector, spearheaded by YPF (NYSE:YPF), as underscored by the ongoing progress in developing the $3B VMOS crude midstream project.

The analyst said it also was reassuring to hear YPF (YPF) CEO say at the company's investor day that its focus is on crude first, with final investment decision on the high-ticket LNG project not occurring before year-end 2026.

Yang also says she is more confident that YPF (YPF) will be able to expand EBITDA to ~$5.2B this year through higher refining margins while continuing to fund its $5B-plus capex plan which is mostly in shale oil; the estimated 2025 cash flow shortfall should be covered by increased leverage, and likely at better rates given the just-announced IMF agreement, the analyst says.

data.title: SA Asks: How will higher tariffs impact Disney?
data.description:
How will higher tariffs impact family entertainment powerhouse Disney (NYSE:DIS)?

We asked Seeking Alpha analysts Jonathan Weber and Daniel Jones for their thoughts.

Jonathan Weber: While some of Disney's business units should be relatively immune to tariffs, such as video streaming, there will be an impact in other areas, e.g. in its (physical) consumer goods business. There could also be secondary effects -- if people worry about a tariff-induced recession, they may decide to forego expenses such as theme park visits, and trade tensions with other countries could cause less tourism, which could impact Disney (NYSE:DIS) as well.

Daniel Jones: For now, the tariff situation appears to be in limbo. There is currently a 90-day pause on tariffs for most countries, during which tariffs will remain flat at 10%. Even an entertainment behemoth like The Walt Disney Company (DIS) could be negatively affected if the tariff situation gets out of control. 

One advantage the company has is that most of what it provides to its customers are services as opposed to products. Total products amounted to $9.52B last year, but that only amounts to 10.4% of the company's overall sales.

The big risk for the company is that the tariff situation could turn out to be highly inflationary in a way that this country and the world has not seen in quite some time. For the most part, Disney (DIS) is viewed as a premium product, and premium products can face pressure when consumer budgets become constrained. It is true that some of the company's revenue is in the form of low-ticket items such as its streaming services. But other parts are at the higher end of the spectrum. 

A good example of this is its theme park business. In 2024, $11.17B of the firm's top line was associated with theme park admissions. Resorts and vacations accounted for another $8.38B. The firm generated an additional $2.26B in park licensing and other fees. Earlier this year, news broke that Disney executives were already concerned that price increases at its parks over recent years have weakened attendance.

According to one source, a family of four visiting Walt Disney World should expect to spend between $2,200 and $3,400 for a weekend trip. For families of six, you are looking at between $3,700 and $5,800. When you consider that the median household income in the US was $80,610 in 2023, we are looking at a pretty significant chunk of a family's revenue for a simple weekend. 

If we are talking about a week, the picture becomes even more drastic. A family of four can expect to spend between $7,200 and $11,000 over the course of a week. And a family of six can expect to spend between $11,900 and $19,000. One source found that 24% of consumers who have gone to a Disney theme park have gone into financial debt to fund the trip. For consumers who have young children, that number jumps to 45%. 

If tariffs usher in higher prices, as they almost certainly will, a constrained consumer could have a negative impact on the company.

data.title: Cboe launches options trading for S&P 500 Equal Weight Index
data.description:
Cboe Global Markets (BATS:CBOE) said Monday it has rolled out options trading for the S&P 500 Equal Weight Index (SP500EW), which differs from the capitalization-weighted S&P 500 Index in sector and factor exposures.

The S&P 500 EWI is the equal-weight version of the S&P 500 Index (SP500) (SPX), with each constituent of the EWI allocated a fixed weight of 0.2% of the index total at each quarterly rebalance.

Complementing Cboe's (BATS:CBOE) S&P 500 Index options, cash-settled EWI options were designed to offer different exposure, including smaller-cap vs. mega-cap stocks, momentum bias, and realized volatility.

The contracts are based on one-tenth the value of the S&P 500 EWI. With a standard contract multiplier of $100, the notional value of an S&P 500 EWI options contract would be ~$63,200 as of market close on April 7.

The launch comes as "investors turn to options at record levels to help manage U.S. equity market exposure and volatility," Catherine Clay, global head of Derivatives at Cboe, said in a statement. 

"We expect these options to cater to both retail and institutional investors looking to diversify and implement a variety of trading strategies, ultimately providing them greater choice and ability to tailor their exposure to fit their needs," she added. "Investors are increasingly seeking to gain more balanced exposure across the market."

Cboe (CBOE) listed Monday standard monthly options expiring on the third Friday. Market participants can trade these contracts during regular hours, though the derivatives exchange expects to also list PM-settled weekly options in the future.

So far this year, the equal-weighted S&P retreated 6%, faring better than the cap-weighed measure's 8.8% slide.

Separately, Invesco's RSP (RSP) is an exchange-traded fund tracking the performance of the S&P 500 EWI.

data.title: DraftKings attracts a bull rating from CFRA on 'attractive' entry point
data.description:
CFRA boosted its rating on DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG) to Buy from Hold on Monday as it sees the company, along with FanDuel, as one of the winners in mobile sports betting in the U.S. The firm expects DraftKings (NASDAQ:DKNG) and FanDuel to maintain over 80% market share. 

Looking ahead, analyst Zachary Warring and his team see DraftKings (DKNG) growing 30% in 2025 and becoming more profitable as the company pulls back on marketing expenses. Notably, DraftKings (DKNG) has no exposure to travel, unlike the large legacy casino stocks. The sports betting is also seen holding up better in a consumer slowdown, which makes it a defensive play within the Casinos and Gaming sub-industry.

CFRA maintained its 2025 and 2026 EPS estimates on DraftKings (DKNG) of $0.50 and $0.75, respectively. "DKNG shares have fallen over 30% since it released its Q4 earnings, which we believe provides a great entry point for shares," highlighted Warring. 

In terms of valuation, Warring noted that DKNG shares are trading at 2.8X near-term sales estimates, which is well below the three- and five-year average. CFRA assigned a price target of $52 to DKNG based on a multiple of 4.0X the 2025 revenue estimate.

Shares of DraftKings (DKNG) are down 8.2% over the last six weeks amid investor concerns about the U.S. economy and the implications of an extended trade war/
